project_name: API Rate Limiting and Throttling System
summary: Implement a comprehensive Redis-based rate limiting system with sliding window algorithm, supporting per-IP and per-user limits, configurable tiers, endpoint-specific policies, admin management panel, metrics/monitoring, intelligent bypass for trusted clients, violation alerts, circuit breakers, and priority request queuing.

user_stories.i_want: to configure rate limit policies per endpoint and user tier
user_stories.so_that: I can protect the API from abuse while ensuring fair access for legitimate users
acceptance_criteria: Admin can create, update, and delete rate limit policies
Policies support requests-per-minute, requests-per-hour, and concurrent request limits
Policies can be applied globally, per-endpoint, per-user-role, or per-API-key
Policy changes take effect within 5 seconds
Admin can view current rate limit utilization across all policies

user_stories.i_want: to receive clear rate limit headers in API responses
user_stories.so_that: I can implement proper backoff and avoid throttling
acceptance_criteria: All rate-limited endpoints return X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset headers
429 responses include Retry-After header
Rate limit information is available in API documentation
Headers reflect both per-IP and per-user limits when both apply

user_stories.i_want: to receive alerts when rate limit violations exceed thresholds
user_stories.so_that: I can respond to potential attacks or abuse patterns quickly
acceptance_criteria: Configurable alert thresholds for violation frequency
Alerts sent via webhook, email, and notification-service
Alerts include source IP, user (if authenticated), endpoint, and violation count
Auto-ban capability after repeated violations
Alert history available in admin panel

user_stories.i_want: my trusted API keys to bypass default rate limits
user_stories.so_that: my high-volume integrations can function reliably
acceptance_criteria: API keys can be marked as trusted with custom limits
Trusted clients have separate, higher rate limit tiers
Trusted status is auditable
Bypass can be configured per-endpoint

user_stories.i_want: to view rate limit metrics and usage patterns
user_stories.so_that: I can optimize API usage and plan capacity
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows rate limit utilization over time
Top consumers by requests and violations are visible
Endpoint-specific statistics available
Metrics exportable to Prometheus/Grafana
Historical data retained for 90 days

user_stories.i_want: circuit breakers to activate on overwhelmed endpoints
user_stories.so_that: system remains partially functional during traffic spikes
acceptance_criteria: Circuit breaker trips when error rate exceeds threshold
Circuit breaker opens for configurable cooldown period
Partial degradation mode with reduced functionality
Circuit breaker status visible in admin panel
Automatic recovery with gradual traffic restoration

user_stories.i_want: high-priority requests to be processed first during congestion
user_stories.so_that: critical operations complete even under load
acceptance_criteria: Priority header (X-Priority) supported on all endpoints
Priority levels: low, normal, high, urgent
Urgent requests guaranteed minimum processing capacity
Priority queue metrics visible in admin panel
Priority-based routing documented

user_stories.i_want: to test rate limit policies before deployment
user_stories.so_that: I can verify policies work as expected
acceptance_criteria: Dry-run mode for policy testing
Load testing tool included
Policy validation before activation
Rollback capability for policy changes
A/B testing support for new policies

functional_requirements.title: Redis-based Sliding Window Rate Limiter
functional_requirements.description: Implement rate limiter using Redis with sliding window algorithm for accurate request counting across distributed services. Support millisecond precision for burst detection and configurable window sizes (1s, 10s, 1m, 10m, 1h).

functional_requirements.title: Multi-Dimension Rate Limiting
functional_requirements.description: Support rate limiting by IP address, authenticated user ID, API key, and combination of dimensions. Each dimension has independent limits with hierarchical override (user > IP > global).

functional_requirements.title: Endpoint-Specific Policies
functional_requirements.description: Configure different rate limits per endpoint with support for wildcard patterns. Read endpoints get higher limits than write endpoints. Expensive operations (document conversion, large file uploads) have separate limits.

functional_requirements.title: Burst vs Sustained Rate Limits
functional_requirements.description: Implement dual limiting: burst limit for short windows (1-10 seconds) and sustained limit for longer windows (1 hour). Allows temporary spikes while protecting against sustained abuse.

functional_requirements.title: Rate Limit Policy Management API
functional_requirements.description: CRUD endpoints for managing rate limit policies with validation, versioning, and audit logging. Support policy templates and bulk operations.

functional_requirements.title: Admin Panel for Rate Limits
functional_requirements.description: React-based admin interface for viewing and managing rate limit policies, real-time metrics, violation logs, and circuit breaker status.

functional_requirements.title: Rate Limit Metrics
functional_requirements.description: Emit OpenTelemetry metrics for rate limit checks, violations, utilization by tier, and circuit breaker events. Include Prometheus endpoint for scraping.

functional_requirements.title: Trusted Client Bypass
functional_requirements.description: API key whitelisting system with custom rate limits. Trusted keys stored in database with associated limits and audit trail.

functional_requirements.title: Rate Limit Violation Alerts
functional_requirements.description: Alert system for violation patterns with configurable thresholds. Integration with notification-service for WebSocket alerts and external webhook support.

functional_requirements.title: Circuit Breaker Pattern
functional_requirements.description: Circuit breaker implementation per endpoint with configurable trip thresholds (error rate, latency percentiles), open/half-open/closed states, and automatic recovery.

functional_requirements.title: Priority Request Queuing
functional_requirements.description: Redis-backed priority queue with 4 priority levels. Fair scheduling within priority levels. Queue depth limits per priority.

functional_requirements.title: Rate Limit Testing Suite
functional_requirements.description: Comprehensive test suite including unit tests for rate limiter algorithms, integration tests for distributed behavior, load tests for performance validation, and chaos tests for failure scenarios.

functional_requirements.title: Print Quota System
functional_requirements.description: Per-user, per-department, and per-printer quota enforcement with configurable limits and rollover policies
functional_requirements.competitive_advantage: Essential for enterprise adoption; CFOs require cost control

functional_requirements.title: Secure Print Release
functional_requirements.description: Card/PIN/biometric authentication at printer before job release; supports badge readers and mobile app auth
functional_requirements.competitive_advantage: Security requirement for healthcare, finance, legal sectors

functional_requirements.title: Cost Allocation Engine
functional_requirements.description: Track and allocate print costs to departments, projects, or clients with detailed reporting
functional_requirements.competitive_advantage: Enables chargeback and cost recovery for MSPs

functional_requirements.title: Mobile Print Apps
functional_requirements.description: Native iOS and Android apps for printing, job release, and account management

functional_requirements.title: Scan-to-Cloud Workflows
functional_requirements.description: Configure MFP scan destinations (email, cloud storage, workflows) with OCR support

functional_requirements.title: Print Job Rules Engine
functional_requirements.description: Conditional routing based on user, document type, size, color, duplex settings; enforce policies
functional_requirements.competitive_advantage: Automation reduces IT overhead and ensures policy compliance

functional_requirements.title: Universal Print Driver
functional_requirements.description: Single driver supporting all printer models with automatic capability detection
functional_requirements.competitive_advantage: Dramatically simplifies deployment; reduces IT overhead

functional_requirements.title: Advanced Analytics Dashboard
functional_requirements.description: Usage trends, anomaly detection, cost forecasting, capacity planning insights

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Rate limit check overhead must be minimal

non_functional_requirements.metric: < 1ms for single key operations, pipelined for multi-key checks

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Rate limiter must handle Redis failures gracefully
non_functional_requirements.metric: Fail-open with degraded limits, < 5 second failover time
IP spoofing detection, X-Forwarded-For validation, signed tokens for distributed enforcement
HMAC-signed rate limit tokens, Redis key expiration, request deduplication

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Complete audit trail for policy changes
non_functional_requirements.metric: 100% of changes logged with user, timestamp, and diff

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Rate limit accuracy across distributed services
non_functional_requirements.metric: < 1% deviation from configured limits under normal load

api_endpoints.description: List all rate limit policies with filtering and pagination
Create a new rate limit policy
Get details of a specific rate limit policy
Get current rate limit metrics and utilization
List rate limit violations with filtering
List trusted API clients with custom limits
Add or update a trusted client
Create an alert rule for violations

columns: scope VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL (global, endpoint, user_role, api_key)
change_type VARCHAR(20) (created, updated, deleted, activated, deactivated)
rate_limit_tier VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'default' (default, tier1, tier2, tier3, enterprise)

risks.risk: Redis failure could cause all requests to be blocked or all limits to be bypassed
risks.mitigation: Implement fail-open with local cached limits, use Redis Sentinel/Cluster for HA, add health checks with automatic fallback

risks.risk: Clock skew between service instances could cause inaccurate rate limiting
risks.mitigation: Use Redis monotonically increasing timestamps, implement clock skew tolerance window

risks.risk: Distributed rate limiting may have race conditions causing occasional limit violations
risks.mitigation: Use Redis Lua scripts for atomic operations, implement request buffering with small over-request allowance

risks.risk: Rate limit bypass via IP spoofing or proxy rotation
risks.mitigation: Validate X-Forwarded-For against trusted proxy list, implement fingerprinting, require auth for sensitive operations

risks.risk: Excessive Redis connections under high load
risks.mitigation: Use connection pooling, implement Redis cluster, batch operations with pipelining

risks.risk: Circuit breaker may trip prematurely during legitimate traffic spikes
risks.mitigation: Configurable thresholds with learning mode, gradual ramp-up, manual override capability

risks.risk: Rate limit metrics collection could impact performance
risks.mitigation: Async metrics emission, sampling for high-frequency metrics, pre-aggregation

implementation_phases.name: Core Rate Limiter
tasks: Implement sliding window algorithm with Redis
Add rate limit middleware with basic IP-based limiting
Implement basic policy CRUD operations in auth-service
Add rate limit HTTP headers to responses
Write unit tests for rate limiter algorithm

implementation_phases.name: Multi-Dimension Limits
tasks: Add burst vs sustained rate limiting
Create trusted_clients table and management API

implementation_phases.name: Endpoint-Specific Policies
tasks: Add endpoint pattern matching to policies
Create default policies for all service endpoints

implementation_phases.name: Admin Panel
tasks: Create rate limit management pages in dashboard

implementation_phases.name: Metrics and Monitoring
tasks: Add OpenTelemetry metrics for rate limiting

implementation_phases.name: Circuit Breakers
tasks: Add circuit breaker UI to admin panel

implementation_phases.name: Testing and Hardening
tasks: Add chaos tests for Redis failures
Run full test suite with coverage

implementation_phases.name: Deployment and Rollout
tasks: Implement feature flags for gradual rollout

key_gaps: No print quota/enforcement capabilities - CFOs require department-level cost tracking
No secure release functionality - security officers require authentication at printer
No mobile printing apps - users expect iOS/Android native printing
No scan-to-cloud/workflow automation - modern MFPs require document routing
No print driver management - deployment complexity increases with device count
unique_selling_points: Truly cloud-native microservices architecture - easier to scale and deploy than legacy on-prem solutions
Open-source foundation with extensible API - no vendor lock-in, community contributions
Modern tech stack (Go 1.24, React, PostgreSQL 16, Redis 7) - better performance and maintainability
Built-in OpenTelemetry tracing - observability from day one
Flexible authentication (JWT, OIDC, SAML) - supports enterprise identity providers natively
Pluggable storage backends (S3, MinIO, local) - deployment flexibility
Real-time WebSocket notifications - modern user experience
Comprehensive API with rate limiting - developer-friendly and production-ready
